title: Irregular Verbs
description: Learn the 'rebel verbs' that change their core spelling when talk to someone directly!
explanation:
**HOOK**
Regular verbs (like drink) are predictable and follow the rules. But Irregular Verbs are like rebels! Just when you think you've learned the 'ich/du/er' patterns, they change their heart. They don't just change their endings—they change the VOWEL inside their very core!

**CONCEPT**
We are learning 'Stem-Changing' verbs. These are super common verbs like: essen (eat), fahren (drive), and lesen (read). They only rebel when they meet 'du' (you) and 'er/sie/es' (he/she/it).

**RULE**
Irregular changes ONLY happen for 'du' and 'er/sie/es'. All other forms (ich, wir, sie-Plural) stay perfectly regular!

tables.title: The Root Changers
tables.note: TIP: E often changes to I (or IE). A often changes to Ä (and gets double dots)!

deep_notes.title: WATCH OUT
deep_notes.text: Don't overthink it! These changes are only for DU and ER. 'Ich esse', but 'Du isst'. Once you get to 'Wir', they always go back to being normal: 'Wir essen'. This pattern happens with almost every irregular verb in A1.

examples.english: You are eating a pizza. (Rebel vowel!)

exercises.question: Was passiert bei 'fahren' in der du-Form?
Er liebt Bücher. Jeden Abend ____ er ein Buch (lesen).
